title: DiffReview Walkthrough
description: Guided review walkthrough of a set of changes, consumed by the DiffReview Neovim plugin. Lives at the repository root as .walkthrough.json.

flow.description: Compact data-flow graph rendered in the walkthrough summary. Use one primary root when possible and branch only where data splits to multiple consumers.

overview.description: Shown before the graph: 1-3 sentences explaining what changed and why.

root.description: Active author-context sentence for the top-level feature, fix, or refactor.

commit.description: Full sha of HEAD at generation time (git rev-parse HEAD). Used for staleness detection; positions describe the working tree as it existed on top of this commit.

tasks.description: Major review tasks. Each task title becomes a numbered review section and Task N.* navigation context.

text.description: One compact runtime/data-flow node label. Do not include a file path.

children.description: Next data-flow nodes or branch consumers.

title.description: Active author-context sentence, e.g. Add..., Modify..., Remove..., Preserve...

justification.description: Optional intent/rationale shown under the task title; aim for 90-140 characters, hard cap 170. Prefer explaining what is now different and why the change follows.

title.description: Concrete owning code boundary such as a module, file, package, directory, crate, or app.
Prose intent row under a group; explains the local purpose served by the concrete changes.

justification.description: Optional intent/rationale shown under the subtask title; aim for 90-140 characters, hard cap 170. Use only for non-obvious local rationale.

role.description: Optional code-proven role label such as Cache, Adapter, or Middleware. Use only when an implemented trait/interface, base class, framework registration, or strong repo convention proves the role; the summary renders this instead of the broad kind keyword.

target.description: Story node or concrete code symbol in the expanded graph.

note.description: Short imperative annotation explaining what this node does in the change; 50 characters or less.

groupType.description: Broad container-level construct for group headers. The summary renders this as a lowercase keyword before the group title.

changeKind.description: Broad programming or review artifact rendered as a lowercase keyword between the action and change target unless role is set. Function and Method both render as fn.

line.description: 1-based line in the NEW (post-change) file as it exists on disk

kind.description: Reviewer-critical callout kind. Deviation is for departures from the original plan; workaround is for a temporary or indirect fix for an issue not fully solved here.

text.description: Compact reviewer-critical context, 180 characters or less. Do not restate the title or comment.

start.description: Start of the referenced region. Prefer a CHANGED (added) line, not unchanged context; for deletion-only comments, use the closest surviving new-side line at the deletion boundary.

end.description: Inclusive end of the referenced region; must be >= start. Keep the region tight around the discussed change (roughly 40 lines at most).

comment.description: Mini justification for this annotation: problem or pressure first, then the solution/change and why it addresses that problem; 1-4 sentences, aiming for roughly 180-200 characters. Do not repeat the file path.

title.description: Succinct active action sentence for the inline annotation header, e.g. Route API requests through the limiter.

callout.description: Optional singular callout for reviewer-critical context. Most annotations omit it.
